mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as market participants price in sustained borrowing costs.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress due to lower aggregate demand.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 156,105 thousand as of May 2023
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with month-over-month PAYEMS changes (Spearman rho > -0.15 over 3-year window), or positive returns occur in > 60% of months with declining PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: When real retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously by more than 0.2 percentage points, this signals a demand shock that erodes corporate profit margins. The joint deterioration triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants reprrice earnings downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines MoM AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 686,573M and UNRATE at 3.7% as of May 2023
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal produces lower Rank IC (< 0.05) than either component signal alone over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month for 2+ consecutive months, signaling supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. This regime-specific mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when combined with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 303), as real returns compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth < 0.5% for 2+ months AND CPIAUCSL > 303
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.01 and CPIAUCSL at 303.29 as of May 2023
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term is lower than either component alone over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% month-over-month, signaling a sharp contraction in residential investment demand. This always-on mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as construction-related earnings and consumer wealth effects deteriorate.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,401 thousand units as of April 2023
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with HOUST declines (Spearman rho > -0.12 over 3-year window), or positive returns occur in > 65% of months with > 10% HOUST decline.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) declines by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month window, this signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates. The mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as cost of capital compression supports valuations.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle or pivot from tightening
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of 3-month periods with FEDFUNDS decline > 0.5pp, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 3.8% while nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) remains positive, this signals a tight labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings. The joint condition predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as full employment supports demand.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% and PAYEMS at 156,105 thousand as of May 2023
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor market
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal produces lower Rank IC (< 0.05) than either component signal alone.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate in consecutive quarters, signaling economic slowdown that compresses earnings multiples. This regime-specific mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when growth momentum falters, as investors reprice terminal value assumptions downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 1.5% for 2+ consecutive quarters
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of periods with sub-1.5% GDP growth, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
